edges.description: The 11p13 deletion obligately removes one WT1 allele, producing WT1 haploinsufficiency.
edges.target: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition

edges.description: The 11p13 deletion obligately removes one PAX6 allele, producing PAX6 haploinsufficiency.
When the deletion extends distally to BDNF, one BDNF allele is lost, producing BDNF haploinsufficiency (WAGRO subtype).
edges.target: BDNF haploinsufficiency and hypothalamic energy dysregulation

edges.description: WT1 haploinsufficiency with somatic second hits predisposes to Wilms tumor.
edges.source: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition

edges.description: WT1 haploinsufficiency disrupts genital development, causing genital anomalies including cryptorchidism.
edges.source: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition

edges.description: WT1 haploinsufficiency disrupts kidney and urinary tract development, causing congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract.
edges.source: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition
edges.target: Congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract

edges.description: WT1-associated nephropathy and the renal consequences of Wilms tumor can progress to kidney failure.
edges.source: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition

edges.description: PAX6 haploinsufficiency contributes to the pan-ocular phenotype including cataract.
PAX6-related anterior-segment dysgenesis raises intraocular pressure, causing glaucoma.
Foveal hypoplasia and aniridia from PAX6 loss produce nystagmus.
PAX6 haploinsufficiency impairs foveal development, causing foveal hypoplasia.
BDNF haploinsufficiency dysregulates hypothalamic energy balance, producing hyperphagia.
edges.source: BDNF haploinsufficiency and hypothalamic energy dysregulation

edges.description: BDNF-driven hyperphagia and impaired energy homeostasis produce childhood-onset obesity in the WAGRO subtype.
edges.source: BDNF haploinsufficiency and hypothalamic energy dysregulation

edges.description: BDNF haploinsufficiency impairs CNS development and is associated with higher rates of autistic features.
edges.source: BDNF haploinsufficiency and hypothalamic energy dysregulation

evidence.reference_title: WAGR Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: a deletion of chromosome 11p13 that includes the genes WT1 and PAX6 identified by molecular genetic testing

evidence.reference_title: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor and obesity in the WAGR syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Heterozygous, variably sized, contiguous gene deletions causing haploinsufficiency of the WT1 and PAX6 genes on chromosome 11p13

evidence.reference_title: Results From the WAGR Syndrome Patient Registry: Characterization of WAGR Spectrum and Recommendations for Care Management.
evidence.snippet: a broader phenotypic spectrum beyond the classic syndrome exists

nodes.description: Partial or complete absence/hypoplasia of the iris from PAX6 haploinsufficiency; a near-constant and often presenting feature of WAGR syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: WAGR syndrome: a clinical review of 54 cases.
evidence.snippet: clinically associated with Wilms' tumor, aniridia, genitourinary anomalies, and mental retardation

nodes.description: Autistic behaviors and neurobehavioral features are common in WAGR syndrome, with the responsible region narrowed to a 1.6 Mb interval containing PAX6, WT1, and PRRG4.

evidence.reference_title: Narrowing of the responsible region for severe developmental delay and autistic behaviors in WAGR syndrome down to 1.6 Mb including PAX6, WT1, and PRRG4.
evidence.snippet: the region responsible for severe developmental delay and autistic features on WAGR syndrome can be narrowed down

nodes.description: When the 11p deletion extends distally to BDNF (11p14.1), brain-derived neurotrophic factor dosage is reduced. BDNF acts in hypothalamic circuits regulating energy homeostasis, and its haploinsufficiency produces hyperphagia and childhood-onset obesity, defining the WAGRO subtype. Serum BDNF is roughly halved in deletion carriers. Because BDNF also regulates CNS development and synaptic plasticity, its haploinsufficiency additionally contributes to more severe cognitive and adaptive-behavior impairment within WAGR syndrome.
nodes.id: BDNF haploinsufficiency and hypothalamic energy dysregulation

evidence.reference_title: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor and obesity in the WAGR syndrome.
evidence.snippet: BDNF haploinsufficiency is associated with lower levels of serum BDNF and with childhood-onset obesity

evidence.reference_title: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor and obesity in the WAGR syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The critical region for childhood-onset obesity in the WAGR syndrome was located within 80 kb of exon 1 of BDNF

evidence.reference_title: Association of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) haploinsufficiency with lower adaptive behaviour and reduced cognitive functioning in WAGR/11p13 deletion syndrome.
evidence.snippet: among subjects with WAGR syndrome, BDNF+/- subjects had a mean Vineland Adaptive Behaviour Compose score that was 14-points lower and a mean intelligence quotient (IQ) that was 20-points lower than BDNF+/+ subjects

nodes.description: Lens opacity occurring as part of the PAX6-related pan-ocular phenotype.

evidence.snippet: lens (cataract and lens subluxation)

nodes.description: Hyperphagia and early-onset obesity occurring in the WAGRO subtype when the deletion includes BDNF.

evidence.reference_title: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor and obesity in the WAGR syndrome.
evidence.snippet: By 10 years of age, 100% of the patients with heterozygous BDNF deletions

nodes.description: Structural anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract (CAKUT) are part of the genitourinary spectrum of WAGR syndrome related to WT1 dosage loss.
nodes.id: Congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract

nodes.description: Genital malformations including cryptorchidism, hypospadias, and ambiguous genitalia, related to WT1 dosage loss; the "G" of WAGR.

evidence.snippet: clinically associated with Wilms' tumor, aniridia, genitourinary anomalies, and mental retardation

nodes.description: Raised intraocular pressure and glaucoma from anterior-segment dysgenesis in the PAX6-related ocular phenotype.

evidence.snippet: anterior segment (resulting in raised intraocular pressure and glaucoma)

nodes.description: Excessive food intake associated with BDNF haploinsufficiency in the WAGRO subtype, contributing to childhood-onset obesity.

evidence.snippet: Hyperphagia and obesity were observed in a subgroup of patients with the WAGR syndrome

nodes.description: Wilms tumor (nephroblastoma) predisposition driven by WT1 haploinsufficiency; a defining and surveillance-relevant component of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: WAGR syndrome: a clinical review of 54 cases.
evidence.snippet: clinically associated with Wilms' tumor, aniridia, genitourinary anomalies, and mental retardation

evidence.reference_title: Clinical characteristics and outcomes of children with WAGR syndrome and Wilms tumor and/or nephroblastomatosis: The 30-year SIOP-RTSG experience.
evidence.snippet: WAGR syndrome (Wilms tumor, aniridia, genitourinary anomalies, and range of developmental delays) is a rare contiguous gene deletion syndrome with a 45% to 60% risk of developing Wilms tumor (WT)

nodes.description: Involuntary eye movements characteristically accompanying aniridia/foveal hypoplasia in PAX6-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: PAX6 Aniridia Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with classic aniridia characteristically show nystagmus and impaired visual acuity

nodes.description: Loss of one PAX6 allele reduces dosage of the PAX6 master transcription factor that controls eye morphogenesis. Haploinsufficiency causes classic aniridia together with a pan-ocular phenotype affecting the cornea, lens, anterior segment, fovea, and optic nerve, as well as central nervous system features.

evidence.reference_title: PAX6 Aniridia Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Classic aniridia affects the iris (partial or full iris hypoplasia), cornea (corneal keratopathy), anterior segment

nodes.description: Kidney failure can develop in WAGR spectrum disorder, related to WT1-associated nephropathy and the renal consequences of Wilms tumor and its treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Results From the WAGR Syndrome Patient Registry: Characterization of WAGR Spectrum and Recommendations for Care Management.
evidence.snippet: patients affected by WAGR syndrome can develop obesity and kidney failure

nodes.description: Loss of one WT1 allele removes a copy of the WT1 zinc-finger transcription factor that is essential for normal nephrogenesis and gonadal development. Haploinsufficiency, with subsequent somatic second hits in renal blastemal cells, predisposes to Wilms tumor and contributes to genitourinary malformations and later glomerular/kidney dysfunction.
nodes.id: WT1 haploinsufficiency and Wilms tumor predisposition

evidence.reference_title: Brain-derived neurotrophic factor and obesity in the WAGR syndrome.
evidence.snippet: haploinsufficiency of the WT1 and PAX6 genes on chromosome 11p13
